title: Linear Algebra in R
description: Matrix operations, determinants, inverses, and solving linear systems

snippets.title: Create Matrix by Row
snippets.code:
A <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), nrow = 2, ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE)
A
snippets.notes: byrow=TRUE fills matrix row by row. Default is column by column.

snippets.title: Special Matrices
snippets.code:
# Identity matrix
I <- diag(3)

# Diagonal matrix
D <- diag(c(2, 3, 4))

# Zero matrix
Z <- matrix(0, nrow = 2, ncol = 3)
snippets.notes: diag(n) creates n×n identity. diag(vector) creates diagonal matrix.

snippets.title: Matrix Multiplication
snippets.code:
A <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
B <- matrix(c(5, 6, 7, 8), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)

# CORRECT: Matrix multiplication
A %*% B

# WRONG: Element-wise multiplication
# A * B  # Don't use this for matrix mult!
snippets.notes: CRITICAL: Always use %*% for matrix multiplication. The * operator does element-wise multiplication.

snippets.title: Transpose
snippets.code:
A <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
t(A)
snippets.notes: Rows become columns. Dimension changes from m×n to n×m.

snippets.title: Calculate Determinant
snippets.code:
# 2×2 determinant
A <- matrix(c(3, 2, 1, 4), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
det(A)  # 3*4 - 2*1 = 10

# 3×3 determinant
B <- matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 7, 0, 8, 4, 5, 6), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE)
det(B)  # 45
snippets.notes: For 2×2: det([[a,b],[c,d]]) = ad - bc. det ≠ 0 means inverse exists.

snippets.title: Matrix Inverse
snippets.code:
A <- matrix(c(2, 1, 3, 5), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)

# Find inverse
A_inv <- solve(A)

# Verify: A × A^(-1) = I
round(A %*% A_inv, 10)
snippets.notes: solve(A) returns A^(-1). Only works if det(A) ≠ 0.

snippets.title: Solve Linear System
snippets.code:
# Solve: 2x + y = 8
#        3x + 5y = 19

A <- matrix(c(2, 1, 3, 5), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
b <- c(8, 19)

x <- solve(A, b)
x  # x = 3, y = 2

# Verify
A %*% x  # Should equal b
snippets.notes: solve(A, b) is more efficient than solve(A) %*% b

snippets.title: Cramer's Rule
snippets.notes: x_i = det(A_i) / det(A) where A_i has column i replaced by b

snippets.title: Market Equilibrium Matrix Form
snippets.code:
# Qd = 100 - 2P  →  Q + 2P = 100
# Qs = -20 + 4P  →  Q - 4P = -20

A <- matrix(c(1, 2, 1, -4), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
b <- c(100, -20)

eq <- solve(A, b)
Q_star <- eq[1]
P_star <- eq[2]

cat("P* =", P_star, ", Q* =", Q_star)

snippets.title: Two-Market System
snippets.code:
# Wheat: 5Pw - Pc = 90
# Corn: -Pw + 7Pc = 120

A <- matrix(c(5, -1, -1, 7), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
b <- c(90, 120)

prices <- solve(A, b)
Pw <- prices[1]
Pc <- prices[2]

cat("Wheat price:", round(Pw, 2))
cat("Corn price:", round(Pc, 2))
snippets.economic_interpretation: Cross-price effects captured in off-diagonal elements

snippets.title: Leontief Input-Output
snippets.code:
# Technology matrix
A <- matrix(c(0.2, 0.4, 0.1, 0.2), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
d <- c(80, 60)  # Final demand

# Leontief solution: x = (I - A)^(-1) × d
I <- diag(2)
leontief <- solve(I - A)
x <- leontief %*% d

cat("Total output:", round(x, 1))
snippets.economic_interpretation: Accounts for direct and indirect input requirements

snippets.title: Eigenvalues for Definiteness
snippets.code:
# Quadratic form: Q = 2x1² + 3x2² + 2x1x2
A <- matrix(c(2, 1, 1, 3), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)

eig <- eigen(A)
eig$values  # 3.618, 1.382

# All positive → positive definite
if (all(eig$values > 0)) {
  cat("Positive definite: minimum exists")
}
snippets.notes: Pos def: all eigenvalues > 0. Neg def: all < 0. Indefinite: mixed signs.

debugging_tips.cause: Matrix dimensions don't match for multiplication
debugging_tips.fix: Check dim(A) and dim(B). For A %*% B, need ncol(A) = nrow(B)
Check det(A). If 0, system has infinite or no solutions
